meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, beginning in French Gulch

image_prompt.subject: a charismatic frontier boss seated in a saloon corner with claim papers, cane, expensive vest, and watchful associates
image_prompt.scene: Jefferson State frontier location tied to the page role, period-authentic 1899 props, dust and weather visible
image_prompt.composition: single strong central subject or tableau, full subject readable in square frame, early-HD western game key-art clarity
image_prompt.boutique_direction: Make him charismatic enough to explain his hold on people and small enough that the reader can imagine meeting him in a saloon corner.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD promotional render crossed with in-engine screenshot composition
render_traits: baked sunlight and hard-edged ambient occlusion
weathered texture maps with modest resolution
grounded 1899 clothing, props, architecture, and landscape
dust, mud, smoke, timber, canvas, iron, leather, and worn paint as tactile materials
style_modifiers.palette: sun-bleached ochre, pine green, oxidized iron, charcoal shadow, muddy umber, faded calico, brass highlights, and cold mountain blue; avoid one-note sepia.

text_rendering.instructions: Avoid readable words; any labels or notices should be indistinct period-appropriate shapes.

self_check: Looks like a PS3-era western game asset or promotional plate, not fantasy storybook art.
Square framing leaves the main subject fully readable at page-card scale.
Every prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier life.
No source/reference project names, no logos, and no readable modern text.
